name: Sant Celoni, La Vall d'Olzinelles – Circular Route
name_original: Sant Celoni, La Vall d'Olzinelles – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Celoni offers diverse landscapes, situated between Montseny Natural Park and Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park. The region features varied mountainous terrain, dense forests of oaks and chestnut trees, and the Tordera river basin with its thriving riverside vegetation. Elevations range from 600 to over 750 meters in higher areas, creating an environment of valleys, hills, and mountain ranges. Approximately 80% of Sant Celoni's territory is covered by vegetation, primarily forests. Best hiking trails around Sant Celoni The most popular hiking route is Sant Celoni, La Vall d'Olzinelles – Circular Route, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through the scenic Olzinelles valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Riera de Gualba Trail (PR-C 211), a difficult 18.2 miles (29.3 km) path. This trail explores the Riera de Gualba and the diverse landscapes of Montseny Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Mediterranean Trail (GR 92), an 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Sant Celoni is defined by dense forests, mountainous terrain, and river basins. The extensive network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Sant Celoni are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Celoni's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sant Celoni?
A: Sant Celoni offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 40 trails available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ensuring there's something for everyone, from easy strolls to challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Sant Celoni?
A: The terrain around Sant Celoni is incredibly diverse, situated between Montseny Natural Park and Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Park. You'll encounter varied mountainous landscapes, dense forests of oaks, holm oaks, cork oaks, and chestnut trees, especially in higher elevations. The Tordera river basin also features thriving riverside vegetation and wetlands, offering a mix of forest paths, valley trails, and hill climbs.

Q: Are there hiking options for all skill levels in Sant Celoni?
A: Yes, Sant Celoni provides hiking opportunities for all abilities. There are 5 easy routes perfect for beginners or a relaxed day out, 30 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 5 difficult routes for experienced hikers looking for a strenuous adventure.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Celoni?
A: Sant Celoni is a great hiking destination year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially on shaded forest trails. Winter offers crisp air and unique views, though higher elevations in Montseny might require appropriate gear.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Sant Celoni?
A: Yes, circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949609993/sant-celoni-la-vall-d-olzinelles-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Celoni, La Vall d'Olzinelles – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.5 km trail leading through the scenic Olzinelles valley.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural springs to discover on hikes?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts natural features like the Dona d'Aigua Waterfall route in Montseny, which combines natural beauty with local mythology. You can also find charming natural springs such as Font del Frare, offering serene spots for a break amidst your hike.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking near Sant Celoni?
A: Hiking routes in Sant Celoni often lead to fascinating historical sites. You can discover ancient megaliths and dolmens, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1296433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Ca l'Arenes&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 5,000 years old. Romanesque chapels, hermitages, and fortified farmhouses are also scattered throughout the forests of Montseny and Montnegre i el Corredor. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1452213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corredor Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, a 16th-century building, offers excellent landscape views.

Q: Are there good viewpoints along the trails in Sant Celoni?
A: Yes, the area is rich with stunning viewpoints. Montseny Natural Park, in particular, offers panoramic vistas from spots like Coll de Bordoriol and Sant Martí de Montseny. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3424011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Llobateres Bird Observatory&lt;/a&gt; for unique perspectives of the wetlands and birdlife.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Sant Celoni?
A: Many paths around Sant Celoni are easily accessible and suitable for all skill levels, making them ideal for families and beginners. The diverse network of routes includes options that are perfect for a leisurely family outing, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Sant Celoni?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive network of well-maintained trails, and the opportunity to explore both natural wonders and historical attractions. The variety of routes, from easy walks to challenging ascents, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I reach hiking trailheads near Sant Celoni using public transport?
A: Sant Celoni is generally well-connected, and some trailheads may be accessible via local bus services or by starting directly from the town center, which has a train station. For specific routes, it's advisable to check local transport schedules to plan your journey to the starting point.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that pass through Sant Celoni?
A: Yes, Sant Celoni is part of significant long-distance routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1328721259/mediterranean-trail-gr-92" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mediterranean Trail (GR 92)&lt;/a&gt; passes through the area, offering a chance to experience a segment of this extensive path. Additionally, the GR 5, known as 'The Viewpoint Trail' ('Ruta dels Miradors'), also traverses the region, providing expansive views.
tips:
Quercus petraea Liebl.
Large-leaved oak
Altitude: 143 m
SIZES (year 2004)
Height: 18.2 m
Perimeter: 3.77 m to 1 m
Crown diameter: 22 m
After the Santa Maria del Montnegre oak, this is the thickest oak measured in Catalonia. Both the rabassa and some of the main branches are covered by insect species protected by Catalan legislation, such as the spectacular escanyapolls
(Lucanus cervus).
The shade of this beautiful tree is used as a softener for the flock of sheep in Can Pla del Montseny, and the shepherd still uses the large salt stone under the tree to put blocks of salt for the cattle. This same stone is commonly used to rest
peasants, walkers and shepherds.

Ice wells were used to store ice produced during the winter and to store it until the summer, when it was marketed. The Can Draper ice well dates from 1771. It is a circular stone construction with a cylindrical shape. It measures approximately 12 meters in total height, but a large part (6.4 m.) Is excavated in the ground. The door of the well is made of granite ashlars and the date of construction can be read on the lintel. The well is covered with a large hemispherical brick dome. The water was collected and left to freeze in a pond upstream. The ice blocks were wrapped in straw and placed in the well. Once full, it was covered with branches, leaves, and earth, and did not reopen until the heat arrived.

Populus nigra L.
Poplar, louse, poplar
Altitude: 195 m
SIZES (year 2004)
Height: 26.6 m
Perimeter: 2.82 m
Crown diameter: 19.5 m
Tree that lives in riparian forests along with poplars, alders or willows. Due to its rapid growth, it is grown in rows for the use of wood.
Its size in height and perimeter make the Olzinelles poplar a remarkable and unique tree in size in the municipality of Sant Celoni. The surrounding wood decomposes dead wood from other riparian trees on which huge stump mushrooms grow.

Altitude: 215 m
SIZES (year 2004)
Height: 35.8 m
Perimeter: 3.6 m
Crown diameter: 28.7 m
The Pega oak is one of the most exceptional trees in the municipality of Sant Celoni. Its height, crown diameter and perimeter size exceed the oaks measured by the government of Catalonia in 1997.
This oak tree clearly marked a stopping point at the source of the Pega on the old road between Sant Celoni and Olzinelles. The house of Can Valls, right next door, had rooms on the body, now in ruins, in case any walker or hauler got dark and had to stay to sleep.

These Ovens are three Large Ceramic containers about 2 MTS High, they were used to obtain the PEGA, a resinous substance that was obtained by cooking firewood 🪵 inside these containers.
This PEGA was used for the waterproofing of Boats, wooden containers, and as fuel for Public Lighting.

The fountain of the Rectory is a bit hidden, just below the church and the rectory. You can go down a path from the path, or you can go up from the road by the path of the pit. This fountain had been hung by the sands of the pit. In August 2008, the City Council of Sant Celoni recovered it thanks to the good availability of the owners and the collaboration of the Diputació de Barcelona. It should be borne in mind that the fountain is fed by water from an underground tank that acts as a well and also supplies the Rectory, so it is advisable not to make it run continuously or excessively.

The church dates back to medieval times, although it has been restored and modified over the centuries. Its history is closely linked to that of the local community and the evolution of the area around it. The Church of Sant Martí d'Olzinelles presents an architectural style that reflects its long history. It has typical elements of the rural churches of the region, with a simple but elegant construction.

Quercus pubescens Willd
Martin oak
Altitude: 230 m
SIZES (year 2004)
Height: 25.5 m
Perimeter: 3.1 m
Crown diameter: 19 m
Oaks and holm oaks with oaks are characteristic forests in the Montnegre. The robust structure of the martinique oak and its strength have made it a particularly respected and admired tree. Wood is used for firewood and furniture, and acorns are good food for sheep and forest animals.
The oak of the Quintà de can Valls is the tallest oak that has been measured in the municipality of Sant Celoni. It grows on an old irrigation canal that ran along the edge of the field, surrounded by holm oaks, oaks and hawthorns.

Pond, well and aqueduct
This pond used to be a laundry room that was located near the so-called body shop, now gone, where the cars of the property were closed and repaired. The pond collects water from the Aranyal ravine, which is channeled through a small stone aqueduct. The pond, the aqueduct, the well at the well and the rows of bananas give this place a special charm.
Bananas from the Aranyal pond
Platanus hispanica Miller ex Münchh
Altitude: 223 m
SIZES (year 2004)
Height: 36 m
Perimeter: 3.07 m
Crown diameter: 22.5 m
Around these irrigation elements and on both sides of the road, grow 26 magnificent bananas that are the tallest set of trees in the municipality of Sant Celoni, as well as the thickest bananas that grow in the municipality.

Cedrus atlantica (Endl.)
Altitude: 230 m
SIZES (year 2004)
Height: 20.75 m
Perimeter: 2.8 m
Crown diameter: 15.8 m
The cedar is a tree that comes from North Africa, in the Atlas Mountains. In Catalonia it is cultivated as an ornamental tree and more rarely in reforestation and forest plantations.
Can Valls cedar was planted in the 1920s. It is the largest cedar measured in the municipality of Sant Celoni. It escaped in November 2002, after strong winds that dropped the last 5 m of the main trunk.
